title: Lebruniodendron leptanthum
taxonomy: Lebruniodendron leptanthum is a species of flowering plant in the family Styracaceae. It is native to China. The classification of Lebruniodendron leptanthum is as follows: family - Styracaceae, genus - Lebruniodendron, species - L. leptanthum. The plant's classification within the family Styracaceae is a part of its broader botanical classification.

intro: Lebruniodendron leptanthum, also known as Lebruniodendron, is a plant species in the family Styracaceae. Traditionally used in folk medicine, the plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cold, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Lebruniodendron include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Lebruniodendron is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat coughs, sore throats, and other upper respiratory tract infections.
